name: Optimizely Content Cloud (Episerver) CMS API
what_it_does: Enterprise Digital Experience Platform combining content management and experimentation — enabling organizations to manage content via Optimizely Content Cloud (formerly Episerver CMS), run A/B tests and feature flags via Optimizely Web Experimentation, personalize experiences, and deliver headless content via REST and GraphQL APIs, with a unified platform for content-driven experimentation.
use_cases: Delivering headless content via Optimizely Content Delivery API for Next.js/React frontends
Managing content types and pages via Optimizely Management API for content operations automation
Running A/B tests and feature flags via Optimizely Experimentation API for conversion optimization
Querying visitor segments and personalization rules via Optimizely API for targeted content delivery
Integrating Optimizely Content Cloud with commerce platforms (Salesforce Commerce, Commercetools) via API
not_for: Small and mid-market companies (Optimizely is enterprise pricing and complexity)
Pure experimentation without CMS (use Optimizely Web standalone for A/B testing only)
Non-.NET organizations for Content Cloud (requires .NET for on-premises CMS)
best_when: An enterprise digital marketing team needs to combine content management with systematic experimentation — running A/B tests on content variants and personalizing experiences with visitor segments, backed by statistical rigor.
avoid_when: You don't need experimentation, are small-to-mid-market, have non-.NET development team for on-premises, or need rapid implementation without enterprise investment.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials for Content Delivery API. Bearer token auth. Workspace-scoped with role-based access. Webhook for content publish and experiment events. Content Delivery REST API and GraphQL API for headless. Management REST API for content operations. Optimizely Experimentation has separate API authentication. .NET SDK for CMS development. JavaScript SDK for experimentation. SAML SSO for enterprise identity.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing starting ~$36,000/year for Content Cloud; Experimentation separate pricing; contact sales
pricing.notes: Stockholm company (Episerver) rebranded to Optimizely in 2021 after acquiring Optimizely (the experimentation company) from Goldman Sachs. Now part of Constellation Software (2022 acquisition). Formerly public (NASDAQ: OPTM). Episerver CMS is .NET-based. Optimizely experimentation is SaaS-native. 9,000+ enterprise customers. Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader for DXP. Customers include Lenovo, Pizza Hut, Electrolux, The Economist. Content + experimentation unification is key differentiator. Nordic engineering culture.

performance.rate_limits: Enterprise rate limits per contract; CDN-delivered content at edge speed

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Content Delivery REST + GraphQL API. Experimentation REST API (separate). OAuth2 auth. Enterprise documentation. Webhook for content and experiment events. Constellation Software acquisition may affect product direction. Two separate product lineages (Episerver CMS + Optimizely Experimentation) with different API patterns. SAML SSO. .NET developer required for on-premises Content Cloud.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Content reads fully idempotent. Experiment reads idempotent. Decision API idempotent for same visitor/flag. Content mutations use content ID for idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Two separate API systems: Content Cloud (CMS) and Experimentation — different auth and endpoints
Constellation Software acquisition (2022) — product direction and investment may change
Implementation partner typically required — 6-18 month enterprise implementation
Content Cloud requires .NET for on-premises; Experimentation is SaaS-native
Experimentation API for feature flags is separate from CMS API — different integration points

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. EU, US, AU data residency. OAuth2 with scopes. Constellation Software with enterprise security standards. Experimentation data (A/B test results, visitor segments) is commercially sensitive. SAML SSO for enterprise identity. HIPAA BAA for healthcare deployments. Personalization profiles require strict GDPR compliance. Content and experimentation data isolation is important security consideration.
